river-man-problem.html.title: River Man Problem
learn: Man's velocity components: vₓ = vₘ cos θ, vᵧ = vₘ sin θ
Time to cross: t = d / vᵧ (depends only on perpendicular component)
Drift distance: D = (vᵣ + vₘ cos θ) × t
Shortest path: aim upstream to cancel drift
Angle for shortest path: cos θ = -vᵣ/vₘ
Real-world: swimming across river, boat crossing stream
